no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states: 'Use the image's natural dimensions (naturalWidth/naturalHeight) relative to the uncropped element size', 'Apply the correct scaling ratio to the drag offset vector', 'Remove any zoom-based scaling', and hints at importing getUncroppedWidthAndHeight. This essentially describes the exact fix rather than just the broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact variable name 'uncroppedSize' (test_uncropped_size_variable_created), exact expressions 'image.naturalWidth / uncroppedSize.width' (test_natural_width_scaling_used), complete absence of 'vectorScale' anywhere in file (test_vector_function_used_directly). An alternative correct fix using different variable names (e.g., 'originalDims') or a different but equivalent scaling approach would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The f2p tests (test_vectorScale_import_removed, test_getUncroppedWidthAndHeight_import_added, test_natural_width_scaling_used, test_uncropped_size_variable_created, test_zoom_scaling_removed, test_vector_function_used_directly) are ALL purely text-matching: they read App.tsx and check for string presence/absence. While p2p tests do run compilation and test suites, every f2p test is string-in-file checking with no behavioral execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check for string patterns in the source file. A stub that simply adds the right import lines and string literals as comments (without implementing any real fix) could pass all f2p tests. For example, adding 'getUncroppedWidthAndHeight(croppingElement)' and 'image.naturalWidth / uncroppedSize.width' as dead code or comments would satisfy the text-matching assertions.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction says 'the image should follow the cursor precisely without drifting' but no test actually verifies this drag behavior. Tests only check source code text patterns and that existing test suites still pass.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies, and are logically grouped into f2p (source checks) and p2p (compilation/test suite) categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves fixing a bug in UI code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, variable names, and commands appear correct and consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact file (App.tsx), the exact function to look for (vectorScale), the exact import to add (helper function from @excalidraw/element for uncropped dimensions), and the exact approach (use naturalWidth/naturalHeight relative to uncropped element size). This goes well beyond identifying the buggy module.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config-edit task.
